description_long: This is a twin-engine, propeller-driven military airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a camouflage paint scheme, featuring brown, green, and gray tones with roundel insignias on the wings and tail. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, with two engines housed in nacelles under each wing. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating a crew of two or more. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era military aircraft, optimized for both speed and maneuverability.
description: A twin-engine, propeller-driven military airplane with camouflage paint, mid-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail. Features roundel insignias and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the camouflage pattern, roundel insignias on the wings and tail, and the twin-engine layout.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin propellers, and the single vertical stabilizer of the tail.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the underside of the wings and fuselage, as well as the engine nacelles and propellers.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the twin propellers, forward cockpit, and the symmetrical wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted in nacelles on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear, configuration not fully visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit likely configured for multiple crew members, with a clear canopy.
